response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and platform engineering credentials, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position carries a hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency, which the candidate does not possess. Beyond that disqualifying gap, the role demands deep embedded C programming, Linux kernel source modification, and hands-on network security protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) — none of which are demonstrated. His C exposure amounts to a brief National Instruments engagement and a university thesis project, and his career trajectory is squarely in web and cloud software, not embedded firmware or LAN-switch security.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience spanning web, mobile, and embedded contexts
Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency systems work using async I/O and performance-oriented architectures at Sociomantic
Solid Linux and DevOps familiarity (CI/CD pipelines, Docker, GitLab administration) providing adjacent infrastructure knowledge
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement and a non-negotiable disqualifier for this position
Zero demonstrated expertise in the required security domain (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption protocols, tamper detection) or LAN-switch embedded firmware
C and Linux kernel experience is superficial and dated, with no evidence of kernel source investigation or modification at any point in the career

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol expertise
